title: cerastium mutabile var. lanatum
intro: Cerastium mutabile var. lanatum, also known as woolly mouse-ear, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its hairy, woolly leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cerastium mutabile var. lanatum include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as a natural antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
